title: QUANTUM_NAV_OS
desc: Real-time operating system for autonomous drone swarm navigation. Implements advanced Kalman filtering for sensor fusion and path planning in high-density obstacles.
overview: A firmware and control stack for autonomous drone fleets operating in high-density obstacle fields, optimized around deterministic task scheduling and low-latency sensor fusion.
challenge: The system had to maintain stable guidance and collision avoidance while reconciling noisy sensor inputs, limited onboard compute, and highly variable line-of-sight conditions.
outcome: Delivered a navigation core with deterministic timing characteristics, resilient fallback states, and tunable flight-control behavior suitable for further hardware integration testing.
highlights: Deterministic task scheduling for guidance loops
Sensor fusion pipeline tuned for noisy field conditions
Fallback state machine for degraded navigation scenarios

title: GRID_SYNC_KERNEL
desc: Smart grid load balancing firmware with micro-second latency. Manages distributed energy resources across large-scale industrial power networks.
overview: A distributed control kernel for balancing industrial energy loads across multiple nodes while maintaining strict timing guarantees and protecting critical infrastructure state.
challenge: The platform needed to arbitrate between fast-moving demand signals, legacy industrial interfaces, and safety-first recovery behavior without introducing destabilizing control lag.
outcome: Produced a kernel architecture for low-latency balancing decisions, clear operator visibility, and safer fault isolation across distributed energy domains.
highlights: Priority-aware balancing decisions across node clusters
Fault isolation logic for unstable grid segments
Operator telemetry hooks for plant-side observability

title: BIO_SENSOR_ARRAY
desc: High-throughput signal processing for wearable medical devices. Features low-power consumption design and high-fidelity ADC sampling algorithms.
overview: A signal-acquisition and processing platform for wearable medical sensing, focused on preserving fidelity while staying within aggressive power and thermal constraints.
challenge: The product needed to balance sampling precision, battery life, and interpretable telemetry output while supporting long-running data capture sessions on small hardware.
outcome: Shaped a low-power sensing pipeline with efficient sampling behavior, cleaner downstream signal data, and better readiness for embedded analytics work.
highlights: Low-power acquisition tuned for wearable duty cycles
Signal conditioning pipeline for cleaner downstream analysis
Sampling strategy designed around battery-sensitive hardware

title: NEURAL_EMBED_CORE
desc: Lightweight neural network inference engine optimized for edge devices. Enables on-device machine learning with minimal memory footprint.
overview: An embedded inference runtime designed for constrained devices that need local ML capability without depending on cloud inference or large memory allocations.
challenge: The runtime had to fit within limited memory budgets, maintain predictable performance, and expose a deployment path that hardware-oriented teams could actually operate.
outcome: Established a compact inference core with predictable runtime characteristics and a deployment model suited to edge-device integration work.
highlights: Compact runtime tuned for constrained memory budgets
Predictable inference behavior for edge-device firmware
Deployment model designed for on-device execution
